Layer 3 · 課堂 7 / 7.1 / 8

Top-down Research Path

Market View → Position → Event → Transmission → Industry → Stocks → Price Confirmation

Alphabet Investor Relations2026-07-22 · Multi-step · Widely followed
Mixed
Market ViewUse the sector map and market-internals lenses as the current market view; a company result does not by itself establish broad market leadership.
Positioning proxyTreat equal-weight versus cap-weight, broad-tech versus mega-cap technology, and risk-appetite spreads as positioning proxies only; they do not establish investor holdings or fund flows.
New EventAlphabet reports second-quarter 2026 results and raises 2026 capital-expenditure guidance
TransmissionHigher planned Alphabet capital expenditure increases the potential demand envelope for data-center compute and networking → The realised effect depends on order timing, product mix, supply availability and customer concentration → Potential read-through to AI infrastructure suppliers requires subsequent company-specific evidence → The next completed US session must confirm or contradict the initial price response
IndustryCloud infrastructure, AI accelerators, Data-center networking
Related symbols$GOOGL · $NVDA · $AMD · $AVGO
Price ConfirmationThe result and revised capex outlook were released after the July 22 cash session; no next-session closing-price confirmation was available at the heartbeat cutoff.

Alphabet reported second-quarter revenue of about $119.8 billion and Google Cloud revenue of $24.77 billion; it guided 2026 capital expenditure to $195 billion to $205 billion, above its prior $180 billion to $190 billion outlook. Current Serenity overlap: $AMD, $AVGO, $GOOGL, $NVDA.

General market research context only; not a score, buy signal, actual position or personalized investment advice.

Cutoff 22 Jul, 09:35 am HKT
$GOOGL23 Jul, 05:02 am / browser_visible

$GOOGL updates FY 2026 capex guidance: To $195B-$205B, up from $180B-$190B. Google is also expected to significantly increase capex in 2027. This is perhaps the most bullish read through on upstream semis from a hyperscaler.

Open on X
$GOOGL23 Jul, 04:45 am / browser_visible

$GOOGL now has 950M monthly Gemini users and processes 22B API tokens per minute, compared to 750M in February. The post discusses constrained compute allocations to $META and others.

Lesson 3 buy-system checks use Pass / Fail / Unknown / Pending with no numeric score.

Lesson 3 shows a pattern-specific Candidate breakout level, not a generic historical high. Lesson 4 assumes entry at that candidate level only after daily-close confirmation; initial resistance and execution reward/risk remain candidate or Unknown until chart evidence is confirmed.
